history: The client spent about a year working on a project in Europe, building a website and conducting interviews, which he feels proud of and wishes to share. He has a past history of procrastination and anxiety, and uses strategies from a course and reminders to manage daily life. The report describes a pattern of sudden aggressive outbursts followed by calm, avoidance of responsibility, and a tendency to externalize blame. Caregivers note ongoing issues with impulsivity, aggression, and avoidance, as well as struggles with treatment adherence and dietary management. The client often avoids engagement and shows reluctance to address difficult emotions or decisions.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
intermediate_belief: If I don't know exactly what to do next, I'll make the wrong choice and disappoint others. If I show uncertainty or weakness, people will see me as a failure. I have to avoid making mistakes or I'll lose the respect of those around me.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't figure out my life direction, it means I'm hopeless and nothing will work out for me. There's no point in trying because I'll just end up failing or letting people down.
coping_strategies: When faced with difficult decisions or ambiguity, the client avoids engagement, procrastinates, or becomes unresponsive. He sometimes externalizes blame or has aggressive outbursts to deflect from his own uncertainty and anxiety. He may also use substances like pop to manage distress, despite knowing it interferes with his treatment.
situation: Facing ambiguity about life direction and choices, with many options to consider.
auto_thought: I have no idea what I'm supposed to do next. If I pick the wrong thing, everything will fall apart and people will think less of me.
behavior: He withdraws from making decisions, avoids discussing options, and becomes unresponsive when asked about his plans. He may lash out or blame others when pressed, or seek comfort in familiar routines like drinking pop.
